overview: Leverage Dragonspeaker Shaman and Savage Ventmaw to rapidly pump Atarka, World Render into a massive, unblockable threat that closes out games before control or combo decks can stabilize. It functions as a tempo-aggro dragon deck that wins by overwhelming the board with a single, game-ending dragon.
win_conditions: Atarka with +3/+3 from Shaman or +4/+4 from Ventmaw.
Sometimes a pumped Ventmaw itself wins.

keep_criteria: Keep hands with 2-3 lands (Forests/Mountains), Dragonspeaker Shaman, Savage Ventmaw, or Atarka.
Prioritize hands that can play a Shaman on turn 1 or 2, or Ventmaw on turn 3.
mulligan_criteria: Mulligan if you have 3+ lands with no threats, or only Atarka with no mana/support.

early_game: Turn 1: Play a Forest or Mountain.
If you have Shaman, play it.
Turn 2: Play a Mountain, cast Shaman if you didn't, or play a 2-drop dragon.
Use Shaman's enter-the-battlefield counter or sacrifice to pump Atarka.
Turn 3: Cast Savage Ventmaw or Atarka, pump with remaining resources, swing.
mid_game: Don't overcommit to board if you can't win the exchange.
Manage mana to ensure you can cast Atarka and pump it in the same turn if possible.
Commit Atarka only when you can protect it or swing for lethal.
Hold back pump resources if the opponent has sweepers.
Use Ventmaw as a finisher or pump source depending on board state.
late_game: Atarka with +3/+3 from Shaman or +4/+4 from Ventmaw.
Sometimes a pumped Ventmaw itself wins.
When you have Atarka on board with a pump source, or when you can swing with a 7/7+ for lethal.
Go all-in if the opponent has no sweepers or answers.

sequencing_tips: Common mistake: Pumping the wrong dragon.
Shaman's ETB counter is free value.
Ventmaw can be a finisher or pump source.
Don't forget to check for flying/trample synergy.

matchups.advice: Use cheap threats (Shaman) to pressure before they can counter. Use pump spells in response to counters if possible. Don't overextend into known counterspells.
Apply early pressure. Don't let them stabilize. Use removal on their mana sources if available.
Play removal, use Atarka as a clock-ender, but be careful of sweepers. Sometimes you need to trade efficiently.
Pressure early, use pump spells to bypass removal. Have a backup plan if Atarka is removed.
Value exchange of pump spells. Shaman is key. Try to out-value them in the late game.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (like Wrath of God equivalents), targeted removal (Lightning Helix, etc.), and combo win conditions.
Note when opponents tap out for answers.
